message:
fix(backpressure): cleanup per-pane drop attribution on PaneDestroyed (review)

[HIGH] Review-pass finding in crates/frankenterm-core/src/ against the
last-20-commits swarm diff. bd8a715e landed BackpressureMetrics::
dropped_by_pane as RwLock<HashMap<u64, AtomicU64>> for per-pane drop
attribution, but never wired a teardown hook. Every pane_id that ever
had a segment dropped leaves an AtomicU64 in the map for the lifetime
of the ObservationRuntime.

On a 200+-pane swarm churning through ~1000 panes/day for 90 days,
that's ~90k entries at ~64 bytes each — small in absolute terms but
monotonically growing, and worse: if the mux reuses a pane_id after
teardown (the exact scenario ft-l6v1r cites in its commit message),
the fresh pane inherits the dead pane's drop count and dashboards
mis-attribute.

## Fix

BackpressureMetrics::cleanup_pane(pane_id) — take a brief write lock
on dropped_by_pane, remove the entry, return bool indicating whether
anything was actually removed. Idempotent on never-inserted panes (the
common "healthy pane" case) and on double-cleanup.

Wire into both existing PaneDestroyed teardown sites, alongside
remove_runtime_pane_state_for_pane (the ft-l6v1r helper):

* runtime.rs handle_native_event NativeEvent::PaneDestroyed arm —
  already has &Arc<BackpressureMetrics> in scope as `backpressure`.

* runtime.rs spawn_discovery_task's diff.closed_panes loop — capture
  an Arc<BackpressureMetrics> via self.metrics.backpressure_metrics()
  alongside the existing cursors/contexts/tracker clones.

## Test

backpressure.rs: cleanup_pane_removes_only_target_pane pins the four
relevant invariants — target-only removal, other panes untouched,
segments_dropped_total() aggregate preserved (it's a monotonic
lifetime counter, not a by-pane sum), and idempotence on both
never-inserted and just-removed panes.

Verified via static inspection of handle_native_event's signature and
spawn_discovery_task's capture list. No runtime validation yet: the
pre-existing web/sse.rs select! macro break (tracked in ft-h9g0q)
blocks `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib` even under
--no-default-features. The additions themselves are pure + localized.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
